content.problemStatement:
You are given a MMM x MMM grid representing a city. A cell in this grid is represented as (i,j)(i,j)(i,j), i.e. the cell at the iii-th row and jjj-th column in the grid. You own two trucks AAA and BBB that deliver goods. Truck AAA is located at at (Ax,Ay)(Ax, Ay)(Ax,Ay) and Truck BBB is located at (Bx,By)(Bx, By)(Bx,By). Both trucks have an infinite number of goods. The two trucks could initially be at the same location.

The distance to travel between cells (i1,j1)(i_1, j_1)(i1‚Äč,j1‚Äč) and (i2,j2)(i_2, j_2)(i2‚Äč,j2‚Äč) is the Manhattan Distance, i.e.,  ‚ą£i1‚ąíi2‚ą£+‚ą£j1‚ąíj2‚ą£|i_1-i_2| + |j_1-j_2|‚ą£i1‚Äč‚ąíi2‚Äč‚ą£+‚ą£j1‚Äč‚ąíj2‚Äč‚ą£ units.

Your company has to serve NNN orders, where the iii-th order is located at position (xi,yi)(x_i, y_i)(xi‚Äč,yi‚Äč). An order is said to be served if some truck moves to the location of the iii-th order. Additionally, the iii-th order can be served only once all order numbers from 1 to i‚ąí1i-1i‚ąí1 have been served.

What is the minimum total distance that the trucks must travel to serve all NNN orders?

###Input:


First line will contain NNN, the number of orders to serve.
The next line contains 2 space separated integers, Ax,AyAx, AyAx,Ay, the co-ordinates of truck A
The next line contains 2 space separated integers, Bx,ByBx, ByBx,By, the co-ordinates of truck B
NNN lines lines follow, iii-th of which contains 2 space separated integers, (xi,yi)(x_i, y_i)(xi‚Äč,yi‚Äč), the location of the iii-th order.
###Output:
Print a single integer, denoting the minimum total distance travelled by the trucks.

###Constraints


1‚Č§N‚Č§50001 \leq N \leq 50001‚Č§N‚Č§5000
1‚Č§Ax,Ay,Bx,By,xi,yi‚Č§1091 \leq A_x, A_y, B_x, B_y, x_i, y_i \leq 10^91‚Č§Ax‚Äč,Ay‚Äč,Bx‚Äč,By‚Äč,xi‚Äč,yi‚Äč‚Č§109
###Subtasks


30 points : 1‚Č§N‚Č§1001 \leq N \leq 1001‚Č§N‚Č§100 and 1‚Č§Ax,Ay,Bx,By,xi,yi,‚Č§1001 \leq A_x, A_y, B_x, B_y, x_i, y_i, \leq 1001‚Č§Ax‚Äč,Ay‚Äč,Bx‚Äč,By‚Äč,xi‚Äč,yi‚Äč,‚Č§100
70 points : original constraints
###Sample Input:
3

3 2

4 2

2 1

2 4

2 1

###Sample Output:
6

###EXPLANATION:

Truck A serves orders 1 and 3 while truck B serves order number 2. Total distance covered by A is 2 units and by B is 4 units. The total distance covered by both trucks combined is 6 units and this is optimal.

One should keep in mind that order number 3 is not served instantly when truck A arrives at location (2,1)(2,1)(2,1). Instead it is served only after both orders 1 and 2 have been served.
constraints: 1 <= Ax, Ay, Bx, By, xi, yi <= 10^9
content.inputFormat: The first line contains the number of orders to serve (N). The next two lines contain the coordinates of truck A (Ax, Ay) and truck B (Bx, By) respectively. The following N lines contain the coordinates of each order (xi, yi).
content.outputFormat: The minimum total distance that the trucks must travel to serve all N orders.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Truck A serves orders 1 and 3 while truck B serves order number 2. Total distance covered by A is 2 units and by B is 4 units. The total distance covered by both trucks combined is 6 units and this is optimal.
Truck A serves order 1 and truck B serves order 2. Total distance covered by A is 2 units and by B is 4 units. The total distance covered by both trucks combined is 6 units and this is optimal.
Truck A serves order 1. Total distance covered by A is 2 units and this is optimal.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use dynamic programming. We maintain two arrays, dpA and dpB, where dpA[i] represents the minimum distance traveled by truck A to serve the first i orders and dpB[i] represents the minimum distance traveled by truck B to serve the first i orders. We initialize dpA[0] and dpB[0] with the Manhattan distance from the initial position of truck A and truck B to the position of the first order. Then, for each order from 2 to N, we calculate the minimum distance traveled by truck A and truck B to serve the current order, considering the previous order was served by either truck A or truck B. We update dpA[i] and dpB[i] accordingly. Finally, we return the minimum of dpA[N] and dpB[N].
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) ‚ÄĒ we iterate over each order once
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) ‚ÄĒ we use two arrays of size N to store the minimum distances

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline
N = int(input())
Ax, Ay = map(int, input().split())
Bx, By = map(int, input().split())
dpA = [0] * N
dpB = [0] * N
x = [0] * N
y = [0] * N
for i in range(N):
    x[i], y[i] = map(int, input().split())
dpA[0] = abs(x[0] - Ax) + abs(y[0] - Ay)
dpB[0] = abs(x[0] - Bx) + abs(y[0] - By)
for i in range(1, N):
    dpA[i] = min(dpA[i-1] + abs(x[i] - x[i-1]) + abs(y[i] - y[i-1]), dpB[i-1] + abs(x[i] - x[i-1]) + abs(y[i] - y[i-1]) + abs(x[i-1] - Ax) + abs(y[i-1] - Ay))
    dpB[i] = min(dpB[i-1] + abs(x[i] - x[i-1]) + abs(y[i] - y[i-1]), dpA[i-1] + abs(x[i] - x[i-1]) + abs(y[i] - y[i-1]) + abs(x[i-1] - Bx) + abs(y[i-1] - By))
print(min(dpA[N-1], dpB[N-1]))
